The Planning and Zoning Commission of the City of Cedar Hill, Texas met on MONDAY, July 2,
2007 at 6:00 p.m. in the Council Chambers of City Hall, Cedar Hill, Texas.
Present: Vice-Chairman Bill Strother and Commissioners David Rush, Theresa Brooks, and
Absent: Chairman Detrick Deburr and Stephen Mason.
I. Call the meeting to order
Vice-Chairman Strother called the meeting to order at 6:03 p.m., declaring it an open meeting in
which a quorum was present and the meeting notice was duly posted.
II. Approve the minutes of the June 18, 2007 meeting
A motion was made by Commissioner Brooks to approve the minutes of the June 18, 2007
meeting, as amended by the draft presented during the briefing session and with minor
typographical corrections. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Rush. The vote was as
Ayes: 4 – Vice-Chairman Strother and Commissioners Rush, Brooks and Saldaña.
Vice-Chairman Strother declared the motion carried.
and consider a request for a IV. Case Number 07- 32 - CONDUCT A PUBLIC HEARING
at 975 Pickard Conditional Use Permit to construct a 100-ft. monopole cell tower
Requested by Dave Green on behalf of Metro PCS. Drive, Cedar Hill, TX.
Page 2 of 4 City of Cedar Hill
Bill Bauman, 2300 Springmere Drive, Arlington, TX, on behalf of Metro PCS
present this request and answer any questions from the Commission. Mr. Bauman stated he was a
representative of Metro PCS and gave the Commission a brief history about the company.
Vice-Chairman Strother opened the floor for anyone wishing to speak in favor of this request.
Vice-Chairman Strother closed that portion of the public hearing and opened the floor for anyone
wishing to speak in opposition to this request.
1. Rosalyn Reese, 911 Steadman Drive, Cedar Hill, TX 75104
Ms. Reece expressed concern that property values would decrease as a result of the this tower.
She also worried about radio frequencies that would possibly be emitted by this cell tower to the
surrounding residential neighborhood and asked if there were any other sites where this tower
Vice-Chairman Strother closed the public hearing for this item and opened the floor to the
In response to Ms. Reese’s comments, Mr. Bauman stated that the actually emissions from a
cellular tower were equivalent to a 60 watt light bulb. He further stated that this site had been
chosen because of the growing need for Metro PCS coverage in this area.
A motion was made by Commissioner Rush to approve Case Number 07-32, as presented. The
motion was seconded by Commissioner Saldaña. The vote was as follows:
Ayes: 4- Vice-Chairman Strother and Commissioners Rush, Brooks and Saldaña.
Vice-Chairman Strother declared the motion carried.
and consider a request for a V. Case Number 07-38 – CONDUCT A PUBLIC HEARING
in conjunction with a restaurant Conditional Use Permit to serve alcoholic beverages
located on Lot 5, The Market at Cedar Hill Addition, more commonly known as 221 East
Requested by Leon Baum. F.M. 1382, Suite 100, Cedar Hill, TX.
Leon Baum, 715 Villa Creek Drive, Duncanville, TX 75137 stepped forth to present this request
and answer any questions from the Commission.
Mr. Baum stated that due to a change in ownership, they were required to get a new Texas
Alcoholic Beverage Commission permit and a new Conditional Use Permit through the City. He
further stated that there would be no changes to the restaurant’s current operations.

A motion was made by Commissioner Brooks to approve Case Number 07-38, as presented.
The motion was seconded by Commissioner Rush. The vote was as follows:
Ayes: 4- Vice-Chairman Strother and Commissioners Rush, Brooks and Saldaña.
Vice-Chairman Strother declared the motion carried.
and consider a proposed VI. CONDUCT A PUBLIC HEARING Tree Preservation
an amendment to the Comprehensive Zoning Ordinance, #2001-64, as Ordinance,
Rod Tyler, Director of Planning, addressed the Commission and informed them that there were
two copies of the proposed Tree Preservation Ordinance in their packet, the first being the final
version and the second a redline version of the final copy and the one originally presented to the
Vice-Chairman Strother opened the floor for anyone wishing to speak in favor of this request.
Vice-Chairman Strother closed that portion of the public hearing and opened the floor for anyone
wishing to speak in opposition to this request.
Vice-Chairman Strother closed the public hearing for this item and opened the floor to the
Commissioner Brooks stated that she appreciated all of the work that has gone into this ordinance
and that it seemed to fulfill the desires and directives of the Commission.

A motion was made by Commissioner Brooks to approve the Tree Preservation Ordinance as
presented. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Saldaña. The vote was as follows:
Ayes: 4 - Vice-Chairman Strother and Commissioners Rush, Brooks and Saldaña.
Vice-Chairman Strother declared the motion carried.
This item was discussed during the briefing session.
A motion was made, followed by a second for adjournment. The meeting adjourned at 6:45 p.m.
